content_en:
al-Hafiz Ibn Nasir ad-Dimishaji, ar-Raddu-/- Wafir [p. 69} said,

He possessed knowledge of the sciences, especially tafsir and usil.

He also said: Aba Bakr Muhammad Ibn al-Muhib said, as found

in his letter, “I said in front of our Shaykh, al-Mizzi, ‘Is Ibnu-l-Qayyim at the same level as Ibn

Lhn Qayyim al-Jawziyyah

Khuzaymah?’ He replied, ‘He is in this time, what Tbn Khuzaymah was in his time.”

As-Suyati, Baghyatu-+ Wi'at [1/62] said,

His books had no equal and he strove and became one of the great Imams in [the field of] tafsir, hadith, the Book, the Sunnah, fur‘, and the Arabic

language.

‘Alt al-Qari, al-Mirgat [8/251],

It will be clear to whoever aspires to read the explanation of Manazilu-s-S@irin [i.e. Madarij asSalikin\, that they [Ibn Taymiyyah and Ibnu-lQayyim] are from the great ones of Ahlu-s-Sunnah wa-l-Jama‘ah, and from the awfiya’ of this Ummah.

Qadi Burhan ad-Din az-Zur‘ said as quoted from him in Dhay/ Tabagat al-Fanabilah,

There is none under the heavens who has greater knowledge than he.

His Death:

Imam Ibnu-l-Qayyim passed away at the age of sixty, on the 13th night of Rajab, 751H, may Allah shower His Mercy upon him.

The Types of Hearts'

Because the heart is described as possessing life or death, it is classified into three types:

The Correct and Sound Heart

This is the truthful and sound (sa/im) heart. It is the only type of heart that a person can bring to Allah on the Day of Judgement which will rescue him. Allah, Exalted is He says,

..the Day when neither wealth nor sons will be of any use - except for he who comes to Allah with a sound and flawless heart.

[ash-Shu'ard (26): 88-89

The meaning of salim (secure) is sdlim (the one who is secure), it has come in this form because it depicts an innate attribute or description of the described. As such it is grammatically like the words tall (¢aw#/), short (gasir), or graceful and charming

(zarif).

"Taken ftom [ghathatu-L-1abfan fi Masaytd ash-Shaytan \1/11-19] of Imam ibn alQayyim, may Allah have mercy upon him.

The Correct and Sound Heart

Therefore the one whose heart is described as sadim is characterised so because this attribute of truthfulness and soundness has become its constant and established quality. In this respect it is like the terms, ‘the one who knows’ (‘a/im) and ‘the one who has power’ (gadir).

It is also the opposite of diseased (arid), sick (sagim) and

ailing (al.

People have described the sound and truthful heart in different ways. However they all revolve around the following basic concept,

[The truthful and sound heart] is that which is secure from every carnal desire that opposes the order and prohibition of Allah. It is secure from every doubt and uncertainty that would obscure or go against His narrative. It is secure from displaying servitude to any other than Him; just as it is secure from seeking ruling from any other than His Messenger (%). Therefore it becomes sound through loving Allah and seeking the ruling of His Messenger. It becomes sound through showing Him fear, hope, trust and reliance, penitence, and humility; it prefers what pleases Him in every circumstance and distances itself from everything that would displease Him in every possible way. This is the reality of servitude (‘ubiadiyyah) which can only be directed to Allah Alone.

Therefore the truthful and sound heart is that heart which is secure from committing any form of shirk whatsoever and instead its servitude is directed only to, and purely for, Allah, Exalted is He. Its desire, Jove, trust and reliance, penitence, humility, fear and hope is only for Allah and its actions are purely

for His sake. Hence if it loves, it loves for the sake of Allah; if it hates, it hates for the sake of Allah; if it gives, it gives for the sake of Allah; and if it withholds, it withholds for the sake of Allah.

But this alone does not suffice until the heart becomes secure from submitting to, and going to anybody else but, the Messenger of Allah (4g) for judgement. Therefore the heart ties a firm bond with him (gg), resolving to follow him and obey him alone in sayings and actions. These sayings comprise the saying of the heart: the matters of belief; and the sayings of the tongue which convey what the heart contains. These actions comprise the actions of the heart: its desire, love, dislike and other connected matters; and the actions of the limbs.

Therefore the judge for all these matters - the major and minor of them - is that which the Messenger (8) came with. Hence the heart does not put itself before him in any matter related to belief, saying, or action. Allah, Exalted is He, says,

ee OR cecert bot bee 7 Sa Bee Sy aya alseol eSB (ak gS Oneeealy O you who believe! Do not put yourself for-

ward in front of Allah and His Messenger... [AL Hujurat (49): 1]

i AN “uN yas

meaning: do not speak of a matter until he has spoken of it and do not act until he has commanded it.

Some of the Sa/af said,

There is no action, even if it be small,

The Correct and Sound Heart

except that two records will be unfurled for it: why? how?

meaning: why did you do it? How did you do it?

The first question enquires about the cause, onset and motive of the action. Was it done for some temporal and worldly gain such as attaining the praise of people? Was it done for fear of people’s censure? Or was the motivation of this action establishing the rights of servitude, seeking the increase of ones love of, and closeness, to Allah, Glorious and Exalted is He, and seeking the means of drawing close (wasilah) to Him?

The essence of this question is: was it upon you to perform this action for the sake of your Master or for the sake of petsonal gain and base desire?

The second question enquires about the following of the Messenger (2) in that action of worship: was the action you did from those actions which have been legislated upon the tongue of My Messenget? Or was it an action that I did not legislate and was not pleased with?

Therefore the first question concerns sincerity (‘Rb/as) and the second concerns following (mutaba‘ah). Allah does not accept any action until both these pre-requisites are met.

The method of absolution from the first question is to putify ones sincerity such that it is for Allah Alone.

The method of absolution from the second question is to actualise the following of the Messenger (4%) and by securing the heart from any intent that would impair its sincerity and any base desire that would impair its following,

This is the reality of the sound and truthful heart from which

ensues victory and bliss.

The Dead Heart

This is the heart that contains no life. It does not know its Lord and it does not worship Him by complying to His command and doing that which He loves and is pleased with. Instead it is a slave to its carnal desires, temptations, and pleasures; oblivious of, and indifferent to whether they lead to the displeasure of its Lord and His anger or not. Therefore it worships other than Allah: it directs its love, hope, pleasure, displeasure, glorification, and submission to other than Him. If it loves, it loves for the sake of its base desires; if it hates, it hates for the sake of its base desires; if it gives, it gives for the sake of its base desires; if it withholds, it withholds for the sake of its base desires. It gives preference to its base desires and these ate more beloved to it than the pleasure of its Master.

Base desires are its leader, carnal desires are its commander, ignorance is its driving force, and negligence is the vessel upon which it embarks. It is completely engrossed in pursuing its worldly desires. It is driven wild by the intoxication of its base desires and love of temporal things. It hears the call to Allah and the Abode of the Hereafter from a distant place and does not respond to the sincere advisor. It follows every cunning devil and the world is the cause of its anger and its pleasure. Base desires have deafened it and blinded it to anything other than falsehood. In this world it is like that which is said concerning Layla,

An enemy to whosoever she displays enmity and at peace with those she likes Whosoever she draws close to,

The Dead Heart

he loves and draws close to.

Mixing with the person who has this heart is a sickness, interacting with him is poison, and sitting with him is ruin.

The Diseased Heart

This is the heart that contains life but also possesses a defect. It has two impulses calling it: one leading it to life and the other leading it to death; and it follows whichever of the two that predominates.

It contains love of Allah, Exalted is He, faith in Him, sincerity to Him, and trust and reliance upon him: those matters that are essential to its life.

It also contains the love of its carnal desires, giving preference to them, and eagerness to attain them. It contains jealousy, arrogance, self-amazement, and love of ranking through attaining leadership: those matters that necessarily lead to its destruction and ruin.

It is constantly being tried by two callers: one calling it to Allah, His Messenger, and the Abode of the Hereafter; and the other calling it to temporal, worldly matters. It responds to the one that is closest and most influential at the time.

Therefore the first type of heart is the living, humble, soft, attentive and heedful heart. The second type is the brittle, dry, and dead heart. The third type is the diseased heart, either it is

closer to its salvation or it is closer to its devastation.

Allah, the Glorious, has mentioned these types of the hearts

in His saying,

PEaaa 247) 9S Al oer fee PAN EIC . 3.

wy

eee BSE 4 oi oy csi 4a Aan oa CES

pe

ad

a

Never did We send a Messenger or a Prophet before you without Shaytan insinuating something into his recitation while he was reciting. But Allah revokes whatever Shaytan insinuates and the Allah confirms His Signs, Allah is AllKnowing, All-Wise. That He may make what Shaytan insinuates a trial for those in whose heart is a disease and whose hearts are hardened. Indeed the wrongdoers are entrenched in hostility. And that those who have been given knowledge may know that it [the Quran] is the truth from your Lord, that they may believe therein and their hearts may submit to it with humility. Indeed Allah is the Guide of those who believe, to the Straight Path.
content_id:
Al-Hafidz Ibnu Nasir Ad-Dimasyqi, Ar-Raddu Al-Wafir [hal. 69] berkata: "Beliau memiliki pengetahuan tentang berbagai ilmu, khususnya tafsir dan usul."

Beliau juga berkata: Abu Bakr Muhammad ibn Al-Muhib mengatakan dalam suratnya: "Aku berkata di hadapan guru kami, Al-Mizzi: 'Apakah Ibnu Al-Qayyim setara dengan Ibnu Khuzaimah?' Beliau menjawab: 'Di zamannya ini, beliau adalah seperti Ibnu Khuzaimah di zamannya.'"

As-Suyuthi, Bughyat Al-Wu'at [1/62] berkata: "Kitab-kitab beliau tidak ada tandingannya dan beliau berusaha dengan sungguh-sungguh hingga menjadi salah seorang Imam besar dalam bidang tafsir, hadis, Al-Quran, Sunnah, furu', dan bahasa Arab."

Ali Al-Qari, Al-Mirqat [8/251] berkata: "Akan jelas bagi siapapun yang berusaha membaca penjelasan Manazil As-Sairin [yakni Madarij As-Salikin] bahwa keduanya (Ibnu Taymiyyah dan Ibnu Al-Qayyim) adalah termasuk para imam besar Ahlus Sunnah wal Jamaah dan termasuk wali-wali umat ini."

Qadhi Burhan Ad-Din Az-Zur'i berkata seperti yang dikutip darinya dalam Dzail Thabaqat Al-Hanabilah: "Tidak ada di bawah kolong langit orang yang lebih berilmu dari beliau."

Kematian Beliau:

Imam Ibnu Al-Qayyim wafat pada usia enam puluh tahun, pada tanggal 13...

Ibnu Al-Qayyim Al-Jawziyyah (Jenis-jenis Hati)

Hati ada tiga jenis: hati yang sehat, hati yang mati, dan hati yang sakit. Hati yang sehat adalah hati yang telah selamat dari semua syahwat yang bertentangan dengan perintah Allah ﷻ dan larangan-Nya, dan dari semua keraguan yang bertentangan dengan apa yang Dia sampaikan. Ia selamat dari menyembah selain Allah ﷻ, dan selamat dari berhukum kepada selain Rasul-Nya ﷺ. Cintanya, bencinya, pemberiannya, dan penolakannya semuanya demi Allah ﷻ.
content_ar: al-Hafiz Ibn Nasir ad-Dimishaji, ar-Raddu-/- Wafir [p. 69} said, He possessed العلم of the sciences, ولا سيما التفسير and usil. He أيضاً said: Aba Bakr محمد ﷺ Ibn al-Muhib said, as found in his letter, “I said in front of our Shaykh, al-Mizzi, ‘Is Ibnu-l-Qayyim at the same level as Ibn Lhn Qayyim al-Jawziyyah Khuzaymah?’ أجاب, ‘He is in this time, what Tbn Khuzaymah was in his time.” As-Suyati, Baghyatu-+ Wi'at [1/62] said, His الكتب had no equal and he strove and became one of the great Imams in [the field of] التفسير, الحديث, the الكتاب, the السنة, fur‘, and the Arabic language. ‘Alt al-Qari, al-Mirgat [8/251], It will be clear to whoever aspires to read the explanation of Manazilu-s-S@irin [i.e. Madarij asSalikin\, that they [ابن تيمية and Ibnu-lQayyim] are from the great ones of Ahlu-s-السنة wa-l-Jama‘ah, and from the awfiya’ of this الأمة. Qadi Burhan ad-Din az-Zur‘ said as quoted from him in Dhay/ Tabagat al-Fanabilah, There is none under the heavens who has greater العلم than he. His الموت: الإمام Ibnu-l-Qayyim passed away at the age of sixty, on the 13th night of Rajab, 751H, may الله shower His الرحمة upon him. The Types of القلوب' لأن the القلب is described as possessing الحياة or الموت, it is classified into three types: The Correct and Sound القلب This is the truthful and sound (sa/im) القلب. It is the only type of القلب that a person can bring to الله on the يوم القيامة which will rescue him. الله, Exalted is He says, ..the Day عندما neither الثروة nor sons will be of any use - except for he who comes to الله with a sound and flawless القلب. [ash-Shu'ard (26): 88-89 The meaning of salim (secure) is sdlim (the one who is secure), it has come in this form لأن it depicts an innate attribute or description of the described. As such it is grammatically like the words tall (¢aw#/), short (gasir), or graceful and charming (zarif). "Taken ftom [ghathatu-L-1abfan fi Masaytd ash-الشيطان \1/11-19] of الإمام ibn alQayyim, may الله have الرحمة upon him. The Correct and Sound القلب لذلك the one whose القلب is described as sadim is characterised لذا لأن this attribute of truthfulness and soundness has become its constant and established quality. In this الاحترام it is like the terms, ‘the one who knows’ (‘a/im) and ‘the one who has power’ (gadir). It is أيضاً the opposite of diseased (arid), sick (sagim) and ailing (al. People have described the sound and truthful القلب in different ways. غير أن they all revolve around the following الأساسي concept, [The truthful and sound القلب] is that which is secure from every carnal desire that opposes the order and prohibition of الله. It is secure from every doubt and uncertainty that would obscure or go against His narrative. It is secure from displaying servitude to any other than Him; just as it is secure from seeking ruling from any other than His Messenger (%). لذلك it becomes sound through loving الله and seeking the ruling of His Messenger. It becomes sound through showing Him الخوف, الأمل, trust and reliance, penitence, and التواضع; it prefers what pleases Him in every circumstance and distances itself from everything that would displease Him in every possible الطريق. This is the reality of servitude (‘ubiadiyyah) which can only be directed to الله Alone. لذلك the truthful and sound القلب is that القلب which is secure from committing any form of الشرك whatsoever and instead its servitude is directed only to, and purely for, الله, Exalted is He. Its desire, Jove, trust and reliance, penitence, التواضع, الخوف and الأمل is only for الله and its actions are purely for His sake. ومن ثَم إذا it loves, it loves for the sake of الله; إذا it hates, it hates for the sake of الله; إذا it gives, it gives for the sake of الله; and إذا it withholds, it withholds for the sake of الله. But this alone does not suffice حتى the القلب becomes secure from submitting to, and going to anybody else but, الرسول ﷺ of الله (4g) for judgement. لذلك the القلب ties a firm bond with him (gg), resolving للاتباع him and obey him alone in sayings and actions. These sayings comprise the saying of the القلب: the matters of الاعتقاد; and the sayings of the tongue which convey what the القلب contains. These actions comprise the actions of the القلب: its desire, المحبة, dislike and other connected matters; and the actions of the limbs. لذلك the judge for all these matters - the major and minor of them - is that which الرسول ﷺ (8) came with. ومن ثَم the القلب does not put itself قبل him in any matter related to الاعتقاد, saying, or action. الله, Exalted is He, says, ee OR cecert bot bee 7 Sa Bee Sy aya alseol eSB (ak gS Oneeealy O you who believe! Do not put yourself for- ward in front of الله and His Messenger... [AL Hujurat (49): 1] i AN “uN yas meaning: do not speak of a matter حتى he has spoken of it and do not act حتى he has commanded it. Some of the Sa/af said, There is no action, even إذا it be small, The Correct and Sound القلب except that two records will be unfurled for it: why? how? meaning: why did you do it? How did you do it? The أولاً question enquires about the cause, onset and motive of the action. Was it done for some temporal and worldly gain مثل attaining the praise of people? Was it done for الخوف of people’s censure? Or was the motivation of this action establishing the الحقوق of servitude, seeking the increase of ones المحبة of, and closeness, to الله, Glorious and Exalted is He, and seeking the means of drawing close (wasilah) to Him? The essence of this question is: was it upon you to perform this action for the sake of your Master or for the sake of petsonal gain and base desire? The ثانياً question enquires about the following of الرسول ﷺ (2) in that action of worship: was the action you did from those actions which have been legislated upon the tongue of My Messenget? Or was it an action that I did not legislate and was not pleased with? لذلك the أولاً question concerns sincerity (‘Rb/as) and the ثانياً concerns following (mutaba‘ah). الله does not accept any action حتى both these pre-requisites are met. The method of absolution from the أولاً question is to putify ones sincerity such that it is for الله Alone. The method of absolution from the ثانياً question is to actualise the following of الرسول ﷺ (4%) and by securing the القلب from any intent that would impair its sincerity and any base desire that would impair its following, This is the reality of the sound and truthful القلب from which ensues victory and bliss. The Dead القلب This is the القلب that contains no الحياة. It does not know its Lord and it does not worship Him by complying to His command and doing that which He loves and is pleased with. Instead it is a slave to its carnal desires, temptations, and pleasures; oblivious of, and indifferent to whether they lead to the displeasure of its Lord and His الغضب or not. لذلك it worships other than الله: it directs its المحبة, الأمل, pleasure, displeasure, glorification, and submission to other than Him. إذا it loves, it loves for the sake of its base desires; إذا it hates, it hates for the sake of its base desires; إذا it gives, it gives for the sake of its base desires; إذا it withholds, it withholds for the sake of its base desires. It gives preference to its base desires and these ate more beloved to it than the pleasure of its Master. Base desires are its leader, carnal desires are its commander, ignorance is its driving force, and negligence is the vessel upon which it embarks. It is completely engrossed in pursuing its worldly desires. It is driven wild by the intoxication of its base desires and المحبة of temporal things. It hears the call to الله and the Abode of الآخرة from a distant place and does not respond to the sincere advisor. It follows every cunning الشيطان and the الدنيا is the cause of its الغضب and its pleasure. Base desires have deafened it and blinded it to anything other than الباطل. In this الدنيا it is like that which is said فيما يتعلق بـ Layla, An enemy to whosoever she displays enmity and at peace with those she likes Whosoever she draws close to, The Dead القلب he loves and draws close to. Mixing with the person who has this القلب is a sickness, interacting with him is poison, and sitting with him is ruin. The Diseased القلب This is the القلب that contains الحياة but أيضاً possesses a defect. It has two impulses calling it: one leading it to الحياة and the other leading it to الموت; and it follows whichever of the two that predominates. It contains المحبة of الله, Exalted is He, الإيمان in Him, sincerity to Him, and trust and reliance upon him: those matters that are الجوهري to its الحياة. It أيضاً contains the المحبة of its carnal desires, giving preference to them, and eagerness to attain them. It contains الغيرة, الغرور, self-amazement, and المحبة of ranking through attaining leadership: those matters that necessarily lead to its destruction and ruin. It is constantly being tried by two callers: one calling it to الله, His Messenger, and the Abode of الآخرة; and the other calling it to temporal, worldly matters. It responds to the one that is closest and most influential at the time. لذلك the أولاً type of القلب is the living, humble, soft, attentive and heedful القلب. The ثانياً type is the brittle, dry, and dead القلب. The ثالثاً type is the diseased القلب, either it is closer to its النجاة or it is closer to its devastation. الله, the Glorious, has mentioned these types of the القلوب in His saying, PEaaa 247) 9S Al oer fee PAN EIC . 3. wy eee BSE 4 oi oy csi 4a Aan oa CES pe ad a Never did We send a Messenger or a النبي قبل you without الشيطان insinuating something into his التلاوة بينما he was reciting. But الله revokes whatever الشيطان insinuates and the الله confirms His Signs, الله is AllKnowing, All-Wise. That He may make what الشيطان insinuates a الابتلاء for those in whose القلب is a المرض and whose القلوب are hardened. Indeed the wrongdoers are entrenched in hostility. And that those who have been given العلم may know that it [the القرآن الكريم] is the الحق from your Lord, that they may believe therein and their القلوب may submit to it with التواضع. Indeed الله is the Guide of those who believe, to the Straight الطريق.
